terminal.c(3) Library Functions Manual terminal.c(3)

NAME

terminal.c - Cross-platform unified color detection and terminal capabilities.

SYNOPSIS

Functions


bool terminal_should_color_output (int fd)
Determine if color output should be used. terminal_color_mode_t terminal_get_effective_color_mode (void)
Get current color mode considering all overrides. bool terminal_is_stdin_tty (void)
bool terminal_is_stdout_tty (void)
bool terminal_is_stderr_tty (void)
bool terminal_is_interactive (void)
unsigned short int terminal_get_effective_width (void)
Get effective terminal width with fallback priority. unsigned short int terminal_get_effective_height (void)
Get effective terminal height with fallback priority. bool terminal_is_piped_output (void)
bool terminal_should_force_stderr (void)
bool terminal_can_prompt_user (void)
int terminal_choose_log_fd (log_level_t level)
void terminal_get_default_foreground_color (int theme, uint8_t *out_r, uint8_t *out_g, uint8_t *out_b)
Get theme-aware default foreground color for the renderer. void terminal_get_default_background_color (int theme, uint8_t *out_r, uint8_t *out_g, uint8_t *out_b)
Get theme-aware default background color for the renderer.

Detailed Description

Cross-platform unified color detection and terminal capabilities.

Definition in file platform/terminal.c.

Function Documentation

bool terminal_can_prompt_user (void )

Definition at line 219 of file platform/terminal.c.

219                                     {
220   // Must be fully interactive (stdin and stdout are TTYs)
221   if (!terminal_is_interactive()) {
222     return false;
223   }
224 
225   // Must not be in snapshot mode (non-interactive capture)
226   if (GET_OPTION(snapshot_mode)) {
227     return false;
228   }
229 
230   // Must not have automated prompt responses configured
231   const char *auto_response = SAFE_GETENV("ASCII_CHAT_QUESTION_PROMPT_RESPONSE");
232   if (auto_response && *auto_response != '\0') {
233     return false; // Automated responses configured, not interactive
234   }
235 
236   // All checks passed, interactive prompts are appropriate
237   return true;
238 }




References terminal_is_interactive().


Referenced by prompt_unknown_host(), and
    server_main().






int
  terminal_choose_log_fd (log_level_t level)


Definition at line 240 of file
  platform/terminal.c.


240                                               {
241   // When force_stderr is enabled (client mode), send ALL logs to stderr
242   // When terminal is NOT interactive (piped/redirected), send ALL logs to stderr
243   // to keep stdout clean for piped data (JSON, frames, etc.)
244   if (log_get_force_stderr() || !terminal_is_interactive()) {
245     return STDERR_FILENO;
246   }
247 
248   // In interactive mode, route based on level:
249   // WARN and above (ERROR, FATAL) go to stderr
250   // Others (DEV, DEBUG, INFO) go to stdout
251   if (level >= LOG_WARN) {
252     return STDERR_FILENO;
253   }
254 
255   return STDOUT_FILENO;
256 }






References log_get_force_stderr(), and
    terminal_is_interactive().


Referenced by log_console_impl(), log_msg(), and
    log_plain_msg().






void
  terminal_get_default_background_color (int theme, uint8_t * out_r, uint8_t *
  out_g, uint8_t * out_b)


Get theme-aware default background color for the renderer.


Parameters


theme Terminal theme (dark or light)


out_r Pointer to store red component (0-255)


out_g Pointer to store green component (0-255)


out_b Pointer to store blue component (0-255)


Returns appropriate default background color based on terminal
    theme. Light theme uses white background, dark theme uses black
  background.


Definition at line 294 of file
  platform/terminal.c.


294                                                                                                       {
295   if (theme == 1) { // TERM_RENDERER_THEME_LIGHT
296     *out_r = TERMINAL_COLOR_THEME_LIGHT_BG_R;
297     *out_g = TERMINAL_COLOR_THEME_LIGHT_BG_G;
298     *out_b = TERMINAL_COLOR_THEME_LIGHT_BG_B;
299   } else { // TERM_RENDERER_THEME_DARK or TERM_RENDERER_THEME_AUTO
300     *out_r = TERMINAL_COLOR_THEME_DARK_BG_R;
301     *out_g = TERMINAL_COLOR_THEME_DARK_BG_G;
302     *out_b = TERMINAL_COLOR_THEME_DARK_BG_B;
303   }
304 }










void
  terminal_get_default_foreground_color (int theme, uint8_t * out_r, uint8_t *
  out_g, uint8_t * out_b)


Get theme-aware default foreground color for the renderer.


Parameters


theme Terminal theme (dark or light)


out_r Pointer to store red component (0-255)


out_g Pointer to store green component (0-255)


out_b Pointer to store blue component (0-255)


Returns appropriate default text color based on terminal theme.
    Both Linux and macOS renderers use this for consistent color selection.


Definition at line 272 of file
  platform/terminal.c.


272                                                                                                       {
273   if (theme == 1) { // TERM_RENDERER_THEME_LIGHT
274     *out_r = TERMINAL_COLOR_THEME_LIGHT_FG_R;
275     *out_g = TERMINAL_COLOR_THEME_LIGHT_FG_G;
276     *out_b = TERMINAL_COLOR_THEME_LIGHT_FG_B;
277   } else { // TERM_RENDERER_THEME_DARK or TERM_RENDERER_THEME_AUTO
278     *out_r = TERMINAL_COLOR_THEME_DARK_FG_R;
279     *out_g = TERMINAL_COLOR_THEME_DARK_FG_G;
280     *out_b = TERMINAL_COLOR_THEME_DARK_FG_B;
281   }
282 }










terminal_color_mode_t
  terminal_get_effective_color_mode (void )


Get current color mode considering all overrides. Determines
    effective color mode by checking:



  
1.

  
--color flag (force enable)

  
2.

  
--color-mode option (none/16/256/truecolor)

  
3.

  
Terminal capability detection




Returns


Effective terminal_color_mode_t to use


Definition at line 112 of file
  platform/terminal.c.


112                                                               {
113   // If colors are disabled entirely by terminal_should_color_output(), return NONE
114   if (!terminal_should_color_output(STDOUT_FILENO)) {
115     return TERM_COLOR_NONE;
116   }
117 
118   // If --color-mode is set to specific value, use it
119   terminal_color_mode_t color_mode = GET_OPTION(color_mode);
120   if (color_mode != TERM_COLOR_AUTO) {
121     return color_mode;
122   }
123 
124   // Otherwise auto-detect from terminal capabilities
125   // For now, return the detected color mode from terminal capabilities
126   // This will be used by apply_color_mode_override() if needed
127   return TERM_COLOR_AUTO; // Let the calling code handle auto-detection
128 }






References terminal_should_color_output().






unsigned
  short int terminal_get_effective_height (void )


Get effective terminal height with fallback priority. Returns
    height in priority order:



  
1.

  
Option height if set to non-zero value

  
2.

  
Detected terminal height

  
3.

  
OPT_HEIGHT_DEFAULT fallback




Definition at line 184 of file
  platform/terminal.c.


184                                                        {
185   int height = GET_OPTION(height);
186 
187   // If height option is explicitly set to non-zero, use it
188   if (height > 0) {
189     return (unsigned short int)height;
190   }
191 
192   // Try to detect actual terminal height
193   terminal_size_t size;
194   if (terminal_get_size(&size) == ASCIICHAT_OK && size.rows > 0) {
195     return (unsigned short int)size.rows;
196   }
197 
198   // Fallback to default
199   return OPT_HEIGHT_DEFAULT;
200 }






References terminal_get_size().


Referenced by keyboard_help_render(),
    mirror_convert_frame(), protocol_start_connection(),
    server_connection_establish(),
    session_display_convert_to_ascii(), session_display_create(),
    session_render_loop(), session_settings_init(), and
    splash_intro_start().






unsigned
  short int terminal_get_effective_width (void )


Get effective terminal width with fallback priority. Returns width
    in priority order:



  
1.

  
Option width if set to non-zero value

  
2.

  
Detected terminal width

  
3.

  
OPT_WIDTH_DEFAULT fallback




Definition at line 158 of file
  platform/terminal.c.


158                                                       {
159   int width = GET_OPTION(width);
160 
161   // If width option is explicitly set to non-zero, use it
162   if (width > 0) {
163     return (unsigned short int)width;
164   }
165 
166   // Try to detect actual terminal width
167   terminal_size_t size;
168   if (terminal_get_size(&size) == ASCIICHAT_OK && size.cols > 0) {
169     return (unsigned short int)size.cols;
170   }
171 
172   // Fallback to default
173   return OPT_WIDTH_DEFAULT;
174 }

bool terminal_should_color_output (int fd)


Determine if color output should be used. Priority order:



  
1.

  
If --color flag is set → ALWAYS use colors (force override)

  
2.

  
If CLAUDECODE env var is set → NEVER use colors (LLM
    automation)

  
3.

  
If output is not a TTY (piping) → NO colors

  
4.

  
If --color-mode=none → NO colors (user choice)

  
5.

  
Otherwise → Use colors




Parameters


fd File descriptor to check (STDOUT_FILENO or
  STDERR_FILENO)


Returns


true if colors should be used, false otherwise


Definition at line 31 of file
  platform/terminal.c.


31                                           {
32   // Check color_mode setting - if explicitly set to NONE, disable colors
33   int color_mode = GET_OPTION(color_mode);
34   if (color_mode == COLOR_MODE_NONE) {
35     return false; // Color mode explicitly disabled
36   }
37 
38   // Get current color setting (auto/true/false)
39   int color_setting = GET_OPTION(color);
40 
41   // Priority 1: --color=true - Force colors ON (overrides everything)
42   if (color_setting == COLOR_SETTING_TRUE) {
43     return true; // ALWAYS colorize
44   }
45 
46   // Priority 2: --color=false - Force colors OFF (overrides everything)
47   if (color_setting == COLOR_SETTING_FALSE) {
48     return false; // NEVER colorize
49   }
50 
51   // Priority 3: --color=auto (default) - Smart detection
52   // Special case: Show colors for --help and --version even if not a TTY
53   // Check for help/version in global argv (set by main.c early)
54   if (g_argc > 1 && g_argv) {
55     for (int i = 1; i < g_argc; i++) {
56       if (strcmp(g_argv[i], "--help") == 0 || strcmp(g_argv[i], "-h") == 0 || strcmp(g_argv[i], "--version") == 0 ||
57           strcmp(g_argv[i], "-v") == 0 || strcmp(g_argv[i], "--show-capabilities") == 0) {
58         // For help/version/show-capabilities, use colors only if output is a TTY
59         // (respect piping convention: no colors unless --color=true)
60         int is_tty = platform_isatty(fd);
61         if (!is_tty) {
62           // Piping detected - fall through to check environment overrides
63           break;
64         }
65 
66         // It's a TTY - show colors for help. Check environment overrides.
67         if (SAFE_GETENV("ASCII_CHAT_COLOR")) {
68           return true; // ASCII_CHAT_COLOR env var forces colors
69         }
70 
71         // CLAUDECODE environment variable (LLM automation) - disable colors
72         if (SAFE_GETENV("CLAUDECODE")) {
73           return false; // NO colors in Claude Code environment
74         }
75 
76         // For help output to TTY, show colors by default
77         return true;
78       }
79     }
80   }
81 
82   // Check environment variable overrides
83   if (SAFE_GETENV("ASCII_CHAT_COLOR")) {
84     return true; // ASCII_CHAT_COLOR env var forces colors
85   }
86 
87   // CLAUDECODE environment variable (LLM automation) - disable colors
88   if (SAFE_GETENV("CLAUDECODE")) {
89     return false; // NO colors in Claude Code environment
90   }
91 
92   // Check if output is a TTY (pipe detection)
93   int is_tty = platform_isatty(fd);
94   if (!is_tty) {
95     return false; // NO colors when piping/redirecting
96   }
97 
98   // Default: Use colors (we have TTY and no environment overrides)
99   return true;
100 }






References g_argc, g_argv, and
    platform_isatty().


Referenced by action_show_capabilities_immediate(),
    action_show_version(), colored_string(),
    colorize_log_message(), log_plain_msg(), and
    terminal_get_effective_color_mode().






bool terminal_should_force_stderr (void )


Definition at line 206 of file
  platform/terminal.c.


206                                         {
207   // If stdout is piped/redirected, force logs to stderr to avoid corruption
208   if (terminal_is_piped_output()) {
209     // Unless in TESTING environment where test framework may capture stdout
210     const char *testing = SAFE_GETENV("TESTING");
211     if (testing && strcmp(testing, "1") == 0) {
212       return false; // Allow stdout in test environments
213     }
214     return true; // Force stderr when piped
215   }
216   return false; // stdout is TTY, no need to force stderr
217 }






References terminal_is_piped_output().


Referenced by main(), session_client_like_run(), and
    session_display_create().
